thesis_title: Authentic Human Social Data as Post-Training Signal
thesis_statement: Training data authenticity, as captured by the proposed L_auth framework, influences post-training outcomes on socially grounded tasks. CampusGo operationalizes this insight as a deployed, open-source data collection platform with verifiable provenance and consent by design. The relative contribution of training-data composition versus inference-time scaling remains an open empirical question.

motivation.label: Background motivation
motivation.scope: Frames why data authenticity matters, but does not serve as the direct proof line for the post-training thesis.

framework.label: Fine-tuning-focused framework
framework.scope: Defines L_auth as a descriptive framework for post-training data-composition effects. Pretraining operationalization is future work, not a validated current result.

primary.label: Primary evidence line
primary.scope: Establishes that data provenance and social-behavioral diversity affect post-training outcomes on socially grounded tasks.

core_contribution.label: Deployed core contribution
core_contribution.scope: CampusGo is a deployed provenance-aware collection platform. Deployment is evidence of implementation, not of validated downstream model gains.

adversarial.label: Competing explanations and honest scoping
adversarial.scope: Surfaces alternative mechanisms such as inference-time scaling, chain-of-thought, and model-scale effects.

beats.title: Model Collapse and Contamination Risk
beats.claim: Recursive synthetic reuse creates a conditional collapse risk. Reactive detection and filtering remain fragile. This motivates concern, but does not directly prove the post-training thesis.
must_cite_counterevidence: pi^2/6 pathway: mixed real+synthetic avoids collapse
Self-correcting loops: correction functions stabilize self-consuming training
Curated synthetic data can work under strong curation
honesty_constraints: Do NOT claim all synthetic data is harmful
Do NOT claim web-scale contamination is proven
Do NOT use Beat 1 as the direct evidence base for the primary post-training claim

beats.title: Partial Measurability of Web Drift
beats.claim: Web drift is partially measurable through proxies and longitudinal observations, but a broad post-2022 contamination audit is still missing.
honesty_constraints: Do NOT claim web-wide degradation is demonstrated
Frame as rising risk and incomplete measurement
Do NOT treat Beat 2 as a substitute for direct post-training evidence

beats.title: L_auth Framework Definition
beats.claim: L_auth is a fine-tuning-focused descriptive framework with four dimensions: provenance ratio, lexical diversity, entropy, and social behavioral diversity. It is a grounded synthesis, not a validated law.
honesty_constraints: Do NOT present L_auth as a validated law
Do NOT present Beat 3 as stage-agnostic validation
Weight calibration remains future work

beats.title: Fine-tuning Data Source Affects Social Reasoning
beats.claim: On socially grounded tasks, data provenance appears especially valuable. The claim is narrower than universal human-data superiority because AI feedback succeeds on bounded tasks.
honesty_constraints: Do NOT claim human data is universally indispensable
Do NOT use pretraining collapse papers as direct support
Frame as especially valuable for socially grounded tasks

beats.title: Contrastive Fine-tuning Experiment
beats.claim: A pilot study compares high/medium/low L_auth data at fixed inference-time compute. Results are directional support, not full validation.
honesty_constraints: Frame as pilot study, not validation
Acknowledge D1 and D4 co-vary
Inference-time scaling remains a competing explanation outside the fixed-compute setup

beats.title: CampusGo as Deployed Core Contribution
beats.claim: CampusGo is a deployed provenance-aware platform designed to collect authentic social interaction data. Deployment demonstrates implementation, not validated downstream model gains.
honesty_constraints: Do NOT confuse deployment with validated model improvement
Acknowledge platform effectiveness for training remains untested
Use implementation and governance language rather than performance-claim language

beats.title: Competing Explanations and Honest Scoping
beats.claim: Inference-time scaling, chain-of-thought, test-time compute, and model-scale effects provide alternative or complementary explanations for social-reasoning improvements. L_auth should be scoped to training-data-composition effects at fixed compute when appropriate.
honesty_constraints: Do NOT dismiss inference-time scaling
Present it as a genuine competing mechanism
Narrow L_auth if evidence favors fixed-compute scoping

citation_chain_requirements.method: Use Semantic Scholar API /paper/{id}/references when possible to verify citation links between spine papers.

narrative_verb_rules.no_citation_but_thematically_related: Use 'in a parallel line of inquiry' 'complementary evidence from' 'independently'

global_constraints.argument_line_separation: Motivation beats must not be used as the direct evidence base for the primary line.
overselling_watchlist: Do not claim human feedback is universally indispensable
Do not claim web-scale contamination is proven
Do not present L_auth as a validated law
Do not treat CampusGo deployment as validated training efficacy
Do not dismiss inference-time scaling or model-scale alternatives
